title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)
summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal LLM that processes images and audio directly without separate encoder models. The architecture eliminates intermediate vision and audio encoders, enabling faster offline inference on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal model from Google DeepMind that processes images and audio directly from raw pixels and waveforms instead of routing them through separate vision and audio encoders. The encoder-free design is the central point: removing intermediate encoders is positioned as the change that enables faster offline inference on consumer hardware such as Apple Silicon. The takeaways are concrete, an encoder-free architecture, direct pixel and waveform processing, and a focus on local inference rather than cloud dependence.
